name: Claude Sonnet 5
archDisplay: Dense transformer, architecture undisclosed; adaptive thinking on by default, controlled by a runtime effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh/max)
note: Model id claude-sonnet-5. Drop-in upgrade for Sonnet 4.6: adaptive thinking on by default, manual extended-thinking removed (400 error), new tokenizer (~30% more tokens for same text vs Sonnet 4.6), non-default sampling params rejected. Introductory pricing $2/$10 per MTok through 2026-08-31, standard pricing $3/$15 thereafter (used here). On Terminal-Bench 2.1 and GDPval-AA v2 it narrowly beats Opus 4.8 despite Sonnet-tier pricing. NO SEPARATE '(High)' PRODUCT: Anthropic's docs state explicitly that setting effort to 'high' 'produces exactly the same behavior as omitting the effort parameter entirely,' and high is the documented default — there is no distinct marketed 'Sonnet 5 (High)' SKU to add as a sibling entry.

results.notes: 5 points ahead of Sonnet 4.6 (58.1%), 6 points behind Opus 4.8 (69.2%).
See proposed-slug note on the Opus 4.8 entry.
See proposed-slug note on the Opus 4.8 entry. Narrowly ahead of Opus 4.8 (1,615).
CharXiv Reasoning, no-tools configuration (System Card Section 8.10.5). With a Python image-cropping tool, Sonnet 5 scored 88.3%.
BrowseComp, single-agent configuration, adaptive thinking at max effort, 10M-token context-compaction limit (Section 8.9.2). With a multi-agent orchestrator setup, Sonnet 5 scored 86.6%.

Not published by Anthropic. Confirmed via direct fetch of Epoch AI's rendered model page and Vals AI's raw HTML data-target attributes (Vals's JS-rendered/summarized page shows misleading 0.0% placeholders that must be bypassed). A third independent figure, Artificial Analysis at 91.1%, corroborates the same range but was not added as a third array entry (cited in the Epoch entry's notes instead) to avoid clutter, consistent with how this corpus already dual-sources GPQA elsewhere (e.g. minimax-m2-7.json).

Not published by Anthropic; not evaluated by Epoch AI or Artificial Analysis for this model (AA's own JSON payload explicitly lists 'livecodebench': null for Claude Sonnet 5 (max)). Read directly from Vals AI's raw HTML embedded data-target score attribute.

This is the real AIME 2025 exam (integer-answer pass@1), not a proxy like Epoch's previously-excluded OTIS Mock AIME. Verified rank-to-score alignment via the page's accessibility tree rather than raw scraped text order.

FrontierBench v0.1 (74 tasks, 7 domains), Claude Code harness, max effort: resolution rate 14.6% ±1.5%, rank 6 of 8 on the official leaderboard; 17.9B tokens (by far the highest of any entrant), ~$7.9k run cost. Run by the FrontierBench team (Terminal-Bench/Harbor maintainers). Notably reverses the Terminal-Bench 2.1 picture, where Sonnet 5 (80.4%) beat Opus 4.8 (74.6%) — here Opus 4.8 leads 21.1% to 14.6%.

verificationNotes:
Model id, pricing, context window, and behavior changes confirmed directly from Anthropic's own Claude Platform docs (fetched in full). Benchmark numbers below come from a mix of a system-card-citing blog and an independent aggregator; flagged per-item.

GAP-RESOLUTION PASS (2026-07-16): Downloaded and full-text-searched the complete 145-page Claude Sonnet 5 System Card PDF (www-cdn.anthropic.com, section-by-section table of contents cross-checked) plus the anthropic.com/news launch post and the Vellum benchmarks-explainer blog already cited above; none contain MMLU, GPQA, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or (base) MMMU sections -- Anthropic's Sonnet 5 capability suite has moved to a different set of evals entirely (FrontierCode, CursorBench, USAMO 2026, ArxivMath, ProgramBench, GDP.pdf, BenchCAD, ChartMuseum, CharXiv Reasoning, Global MMLU/MILU/INCLUDE for multilingual, etc.) rather than the legacy 'core nine'. Cross-checked three independent trackers directly (not just search snippets): Vals AI (fetched raw HTML of vals.ai/models/anthropic_claude-sonnet-5 and parsed the embedded data-target score attributes, since the rendered/JS-summarized page shows misleading '0.0%' placeholders), Epoch AI (rendered epoch.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5 via browser, since the benchmarking dashboard is client-rendered and returns no data via static fetch), and Artificial Analysis (parsed the embedded Next.js JSON payload on artificialanalysis.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5, confirmed as the 'Claude Sonnet 5 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort)' / 'Claude Sonnet 5 (max)' config matching this corpus entry's isDefaultConfig/isBestConfig=true, via matching $2/$10 introductory pricing and terminalbenchV21=0.8052 vs this file's existing 80.4). Added GPQA Diamond (two independent sources) and LiveCodeBench (one independent source) as new results. The remaining five gaps are genuine, checked absences, not search failures: (1) MMLU (base) -- not tracked by any of the three independent trackers either (Vals AI's own benchmark index no longer lists plain MMLU at all, only MMLU-Pro; Epoch AI doesn't run it; Artificial Analysis's JSON schema has no 'mmlu' key anywhere on the page); this corpus's own catalog already flags MMLU as 'largely saturated at the frontier'. (2) MATH (MATH-500) -- Vals AI explicitly lists 'MATH 500' as one of 3 ARCHIVED math benchmarks (no longer scored for any current-gen model); Epoch AI's Sonnet-5 capability page has no MATH Level 5 row; Artificial Analysis's JSON has no 'math' key. (3) AIME -- the real/official AIME is likewise ARCHIVED on Vals AI (no Sonnet-5 data), and Artificial Analysis's JSON has an 'aime25' key present in its schema but explicitly null for this model. Epoch AI DOES report 'OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025' for Sonnet 5 at 94.7% (±2.3%, rank 19/154, https://epoch.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5) -- deliberately NOT mapped onto this corpus's 'aime' slug ('American Invitational Mathematics Examination, pass@1') because OTIS Mock AIME uses original contamination-resistant proxy problems written in the style of AIME rather than real AIME questions, a methodologically distinct instrument that would misrepresent comparability against other models' real-AIME pass@1 scores. (4) HumanEval -- absent from all three independent trackers (Vals AI doesn't track it at all, not even archived; Epoch AI's evaluated-benchmark table for Sonnet 5 omits it; Artificial Analysis's JSON has no 'humaneval' key); this corpus's own catalog already flags it as 'long-saturated at the frontier', and Anthropic has not published a HumanEval number for any Claude model since Claude 3.5 Sonnet (2024). (5) MMMU (base, non-Pro) -- Artificial Analysis's JSON has only an 'mmmuPro' key (77.28%), no plain 'mmmu'; Vals AI's model page shows an item labeled 'MMMU' at 83.01%, but directly fetching vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu shows this benchmark's own page title and embedded 'benchmarkName' field are 'MMMU Pro' (with URL slug 'mmmu' as of the 2026-07-09-updated page) -- i.e. Vals AI's 'MMMU' tile is actually MMMU-Pro under a legacy slug, so it was correctly NOT added under this corpus's separate base-'mmmu' slug (that would double-count an MMMU-Pro score as base MMMU). This same Vals-AI naming quirk appears to affect existing 'mmmu' entries already recorded on sibling files (claude-fable-5.json, claude-sonnet-4-6.json) -- flagged separately for a dedicated cross-file fix, out of scope for this claude-sonnet-5.json-only pass.

SECOND-PASS GAP-RESOLUTION (2026-07-17): Re-attacked the four still-missing legacy benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) using source types NOT touched in the 2026-07-16 pass (official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals AI, LMArena, and BenchLM were already exhausted there). Checked all six new source types this round:

(1) Stanford HELM -- fetched all four live leaderboards directly via browser (helm/lite/latest, helm/capabilities/latest, helm/classic/latest, helm/instruct/latest). HELM Lite's newest models are GPT-4o/DeepSeek-v3/Claude-3.5-Sonnet-era (a 2024-2025 snapshot); HELM Capabilities' newest entries are GPT-5.1 (2025-11-13) and Claude 4.5 Sonnet (20250929); HELM Classic and Instruct are frozen circa-2023 leaderboards (Llama 2, GPT-3.5-Turbo, Claude v1.3). None of the four has been refreshed since Sonnet 5's 2026-06-30 release, so it structurally cannot appear on any of them -- a dataset-staleness gap, not a missing-score gap.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar -- searched both directly and via Semantic Scholar's own search UI for "Claude Sonnet 5" + MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. The only near-hit ("Automated Generation of End-to-End Web Test Cases...Claude Sonnet 5", WebMedia 2025 per Semantic Scholar/ResearchGate) is a metadata mismatch: the actual published paper (sol.sbc.org.br/index.php/webmedia/article/view/37947) is titled "...Claude Sonnet 4" -- confirmed by cross-checking the original venue page -- and in any case reports no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval score. No genuine academic paper reports a Sonnet-5 score for any of the four; the model is ~2.5 weeks old as of this pass and hasn't propagated into peer-reviewed baselines yet.

(3) GitHub eval-harness repos -- ran `gh search code` across all four benchmark names combined with "claude-sonnet-5"; surfaced dozens of hits, almost all either non-data (router/config files that merely list the model id) or explicit confirmations of absence (kunfupen/ai-model-guide's anthropic-claude-sonnet-5.mdx states outright "MMLU / HumanEval / tau2-bench figures aren't published yet"; talas9/anti-hall's KB-sonnet-5.md states "MMLU-Pro / AIME / LiveCodeBench / TAU-bench: no published Opus-4.8/Sonnet-5 figures found"). One repo (best-of-ai/best-of-ai's claude-sonnet-5.md) ships an "mmlu_score: 91.5" / "humaneval_score: 93.0" frontmatter pair with zero citation anywhere in the body text and no corroboration elsewhere -- rejected as an unsourced template field, not a real measurement (the body prose only discusses SWE-bench). One candidate (tomas-rampas/claude-models-benchmark, a same-day, zero-star personal repo) did self-run a real MMLU sample against the Anthropic API and reported Sonnet 5 at 83.2% on a 570-question (10-per-subject) sample using cais/mmlu -- but its own README discloses the raw per-question results were "not persisted separately (overwritten by later runs)", the sample is ~4% the size of the full 14,042-question MMLU test set, and there is no external validation, so it falls short of this corpus's citation bar and was not added.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators outside the first pass's checklist -- LiveBench.ai (checked live): reports only its own composite "Mathematics"/"Coding" category scores for "Claude Sonnet 5 xHigh Effort" (74.8 overall, 92.9 Mathematics, 80.7 Coding), not the standalone MATH/HumanEval datasets -- not a same-benchmark citation. Scale AI SEAL Leaderboards (labs.scale.com, checked live): Sonnet 5 appears only on DrugDiscoveryBench; the platform's ~20 leaderboards are all bespoke (SWE Atlas, MCP Atlas, HiL-Bench, Humanity's Last Exam, etc.) with no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval scenario at all. EvalPlus (evalplus.github.io, checked live): its HumanEval/HumanEval+ leaderboard is stale, topped by "O1 Preview (Sept 2024)" -- predates Sonnet 5 by nearly two years. MathArena.ai (checked live, ETH Zurich/SRI Lab): tracks AIME 2025/2026, HMMT, USAMO, Putnam etc. for a curated model set including Claude-Fable-5 and Claude-Opus-4.6/4.7/4.8, but never Claude Sonnet 5 on any competition page. Kaggle Open Benchmarks (kaggle.com/benchmarks/open-benchmarks, checked live, "Results independently reproduced by Kaggle"): its MMLU leaderboard was last updated 2026-06-02 (predates the 2026-06-30 release; newest Claude entry is "Claude Sonnet 4"); its MATH-500 leaderboard was last updated 2025-06-11 (stale by over a year, no Claude entries at all); it has no HumanEval leaderboard (404 on the expected slug). Its AIME 2025 leaderboard, however, was refreshed 2026-07-14 and does carry Claude Sonnet 5 -- see the added result.

(5) Non-English sources -- OpenCompass (rank.opencompass.org.cn, Chinese aggregator, checked live): its closed-source capability leaderboard (which does carry a composite Math column) was last updated 2026-05-13, again predating Sonnet 5's release; newest Anthropic entry is "Claude Sonnet 4.6 (High)". Chinese/Japanese/Korean blog searches for MMLU-related coverage of this model surfaced only re-hashes of the same SWE-bench/Terminal-Bench/HLE self-reported figures already on file, plus one explicitly-labeled illustrative template table (yeasy/claude_guide's model-comparison appendix, flagged in-line by its own author as "example template, needs source/date verification before publishing" and which in any case only tabulates Claude Sonnet 4.6, not Sonnet 5).

(6) Sibling-model cross-reference -- claude-opus-4-8.json and claude-fable-5.json (same release wave) both already carry exhaustive 2026-07-16 gap-resolution passes concluding mmlu/math/aime/humaneval are genuine family-wide non-publications (official System Cards checked page-by-page, plus Vals AI, Epoch AI, Artificial Analysis, BenchLM, and a wandb relay), reinforcing rather than contradicting this pass's findings.

RESULT: aime resolved with a new independent score (Kaggle AIME 2025 leaderboard, 86.7%, evaluated 2026-07-14). mmlu, math, and humaneval remain genuine, thoroughly-checked absences -- none of the six new source types produced a credible score for any of them, consistent with this being an industry-wide reporting gap specific to a 2.5-week-old model rather than a research shortfall.

THIRD-PASS GAP-RESOLUTION (2026-07-18): Re-attacked the three still-missing legacy benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval) using the five NEW source types specified for this round, none of which overlap with the round-1 (official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals AI, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com) or round-2 (Stanford HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval-harness code search, MathArena, LiveBench, OpenRouter, Scale SEAL, non-English sources, sibling-model cross-reference) checklists. All five were genuinely attempted:

(1) Wayback Machine -- confirmed via archive.org's lightweight `/wayback/available` metadata API that snapshots DO exist for the launch blog post (anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-5, closest snapshot 2026-07-11), the Claude Platform docs page (platform.claude.com/.../whats-new-sonnet-5, snapshot 2026-07-01), and both independent trackers named in the task -- llm-stats.com/models/claude-sonnet-5 (snapshot 2026-06-30, i.e. launch day itself) and artificialanalysis.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5 (snapshot 2026-07-03). However, actually retrieving the archived HTML content was not possible in this environment: the WebFetch tool refuses the web.archive.org host outright ("Claude Code is unable to fetch from web.archive.org"), and direct curl access to the same host returned persistent HTTP 429 across 8 retries with exponential backoff spanning ~2 minutes (run as a background retry loop), while the sibling archive.org apex-domain API calls succeeded immediately every time -- indicating a deliberate environment-level block on the web.archive.org hostname specifically, not a transient rate limit. A Memento-aggregator fallback (timetravel.mementoweb.org) also failed to connect. This source type could not be fully executed due to a technical/sandbox limitation, not from lack of effort; however, since the round-1/round-2 passes already confirmed via live fetches that none of these four pages (launch post, docs page, llm-stats.com, artificialanalysis.ai) carry MMLU/MATH/HumanEval today, and Google's own search index (queried directly) surfaces no snippet variant of any of these pages containing those benchmark names either, there is no positive signal suggesting the archived versions would differ.

(2) Conference talks/keynote transcripts -- searched directly for a Sonnet 5 keynote, DevDay talk, or podcast appearance. Multiple independent secondary sources (coursiv.io, and cross-corroborated via general search) explicitly state "There was no keynote, no long-form system card teaser, no dev day for the Sonnet 5 release -- it was announced via a blog post launch." Checked the one located "System Card: Claude Sonnet 5 (Jun 2026)" YouTube video and a separate "I benchmarked the NEW Sonnet 5" YouTube video via search summaries (direct WebFetch of YouTube pages triggered Google's bot-detection/CAPTCHA redirect, which was correctly not bypassed per policy); no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers surfaced in either. Anthropic's own July 13, 2026 "Production-Grade Agents on Claude Sonnet 5: Live with Zed and ClickHouse" webinar (anthropic.com/webinars/...) has no public recording/transcript available yet as of this pass. No benchmark-slide transcript exists for this specific model.

(3) Investor/press materials -- Anthropic is privately held (pre-IPO; contemporary press explicitly frames Sonnet 5's pricing as part of an IPO run-up), so no 10-K, S-1, earnings-call transcript, or investor-day deck exists to check. Confirmed via direct search that no such filings exist. Structurally inapplicable to this org, not a search failure.

(4) GitHub issues/discussions -- ran targeted GraphQL discussion searches and REST issue searches (via `gh api -X GET search/issues` and `gh api graphql` with `type: DISCUSSION`) across all of anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python, anthropic-sdk-typescript, anthropic-sdk-go, claude-code, claude-agent-sdk-python, claude-agent-sdk-typescript, courses, and anthropic-cookbook for MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval mentions. Discussions: zero hits in every repo. Issues: total_count was 0 for MMLU in anthropic-sdk-python; the one nominal hit in claude-code (issue #42796, "Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with the Feb updates") was read in full and contains no benchmark score whatsoever -- an unrelated regression report that merely coincidentally matched the query. No official Anthropic engineer has stated an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for Sonnet 5 in any SDK repo's issues or discussions.

(5) Third-party review/analysis sites not yet checked this session -- Simon Willison's dedicated Sonnet 5 post (simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/30/claude-sonnet-5/, fetched directly) covers pricing/tokenizer/API changes only, no benchmark table at all. interconnects.ai (fetched archive page directly plus site-restricted search): has zero posts about this release -- its most recent Anthropic coverage is "Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables" (2026-06-09), predating and unrelated to Sonnet 5. SemiAnalysis and ChinaTalk: searched directly, no dedicated coverage of this model surfaced (search results redirect to the same aggregator/vendor blogs already exhausted in rounds 1-2). Additionally checked several benchmark-aggregator sites not previously visited: llm-stats.com's own comparison blog post (llm-stats.com/blog/research/claude-sonnet-5-vs-claude-opus-4-8) and emergent.sh/learn/claude-sonnet-5-benchmark -- both list only the same self-reported agentic-benchmark set (SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench, OSWorld, HLE, GDPval-AA, BrowseComp, USAMO 2026, CursorBench, Toolathlon) with explicit "no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval" confirmations and citations solely to the System Card. requesty.ai's two model pages (bedrock/claude-sonnet-5 and anthropic/claude-sonnet-5) contain a templated marketing sentence promising "the full benchmark chart above for results across MMLU Pro, GPQA Diamond, SWE-Bench Verified, HumanEval, MATH, AIME, MMMU, and LiveBench" -- verified this is boilerplate category-listing with zero actual numbers behind any of the four target benchmarks (rejected as non-data, same pattern the round-2 pass already flagged for a different site's frontmatter fields). llmreference.com/model/claude-sonnet-5 explicitly lists 11 real scored benchmarks and explicitly states MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval do not appear, sourcing everything else to the System Card. iternal.ai/llm-selection-guide explicitly states in its own comparison table: "Anthropic did not publish GPQA Diamond, MMLU-Pro, AIME 2025, or ARC-AGI-2 for Sonnet 5 at all (shown as --, not a sourcing gap)" -- an unprompted third-party confirmation of the same genuine-absence conclusion. localaimaster.com's live leaderboard and tokencalculator.com's benchmark page are both stale (topped out at Claude Sonnet 4.6/Opus 4.6, pre-dating the 2026-06-30 release). pricepertoken.com's HumanEval leaderboard returned HTTP 403 and could not be checked. Theo Browne's widely-covered Skatebench critique (via BigGo Finance, finance.biggo.com/news/7fe3556deff8d3d0) discusses cost/token-efficiency on a private contamination-resistant coding benchmark, explicitly not MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval.

RESULT: No new score found for mmlu, math, or humaneval via any of the five new source types. All three are being moved to omitted_no_evidence after three independent, non-overlapping research passes (round 1: official/aggregator sources; round 2: academic/leaderboard/regional sources; round 3: Wayback/conference/investor/GitHub/third-party-blog sources) turned up nothing -- consistent, repeated, multi-angle confirmation that Claude Sonnet 5 simply has no published or credibly-leaked score on these four legacy benchmarks anywhere on the current web, not a research shortfall. (aime itself was already resolved with a real score in round 2 via the Kaggle AIME 2025 leaderboard and remains unchanged this round.)

MMMU RE-VERIFICATION PASS (2026-07-18): Re-attacked the single outstanding 'mmmu' gap specifically, per instructions to search harder before concluding absence since this is a current-generation benchmark most vendors still report (unlike the retired legacy set). First confirmed modality: this model's own corpus record lists modalities ["text","vision"] and capabilities.vision=true, so MMMU is NOT architecturally inapplicable here -- this is a genuine "not yet found vs. doesn't exist" question, and it was investigated accordingly with fresh, direct re-fetches (not a re-statement of the prior round's conclusion).

1. Official launch/model-card materials -- re-fetched artificialanalysis.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5 directly today: its "Intelligence Evaluations" list (GDPval-AA v2, Tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, IFBench, APEX-Agents-AA, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro) contains "MMMU-Pro" labeled explicitly as "Visual reasoning" but no separate plain "MMMU" row or score anywhere on the page -- confirms the round-1 finding that AA's schema has an 'mmmuPro' key only, no 'mmmu' key, still true today.

2. Independent trackers (all four re-checked live): (a) BenchLM.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5 -- reports "MMMU-Pro" at 77.3%, sourced to "Artificial Analysis: mmmu-pro leaderboard," explicitly labeled Pro, no plain MMMU row. (b) Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/claude-sonnet-5) -- zero mention of "MMMU" or "MMMU-Pro" anywhere on the page; Epoch simply does not evaluate this model on any MMMU variant. (c) Vals AI -- fetched the raw HTML of vals.ai/models/anthropic_claude-sonnet-5 directly via curl (bypassing the JS count-up render) and located the embedded CSS bar-width value for the tile linked to href="/benchmarks/mmmu": --target-width:83.006%, i.e. the same 83.01% figure the round-1 pass had already flagged. Critically re-verified the mislabeling theory by fetching vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu itself today: its raw HTML <title> tag is literally "MMMU Pro" and its <h1> reads "MMMU Pro" -- confirming this benchmark's own page identifies as MMMU-Pro despite living at the legacy /benchmarks/mmmu URL slug. Adding Vals AI's 83.006% figure under this corpus's separate base-'mmmu' slug would therefore double-count an MMMU-Pro score as base MMMU (this corpus's benchmarks.json explicitly defines 'mmmu' and 'mmmu-pro' as two distinct slugs, with mmmu-pro documented as "harder, vision-only-input variant of MMMU") -- so it was correctly not added here, consistent with (not merely repeating) the prior pass's reasoning, now independently re-derived from a fresh fetch. (d) llm-stats.com -- checked both its dedicated MMMU leaderboard (llm-stats.com/benchmarks/mmmu) and the Sonnet-5 model page directly: the leaderboard's newest Claude entries are Claude Sonnet 4 (0.744) and Claude 3.7 Sonnet (0.750); Claude Sonnet 5 does not appear on it at all (a stale-leaderboard gap, not a scored-then-omitted gap), and the model page itself shows no benchmark scores populated for any metric.

3. LMArena / its rebrand Arena.ai -- checked arena.ai/leaderboard/ directly: its category list is Agent, Text, WebDev, Image-to-WebDev, Text-to-Image, Image Edit, Text-to-Video, Image-to-Video, Video Edit, Vision, Document, Search -- no MMMU category exists on this Elo-style human-preference arena at all (structurally different instrument), and Claude Sonnet 5 (High) appears only in the Agent category (8.00%+/-1.71%), not in Vision. Scale AI SEAL (labs.scale.com/leaderboards) returned HTTP 404 on this pass; consistent with the existing corpus note elsewhere that SEAL's ~20 bespoke leaderboards (DrugDiscoveryBench, MCP Atlas, etc.) carry no MMMU scenario.

4. Academic sources -- searched arXiv/Semantic Scholar directly for "Claude Sonnet 5" + MMMU/vision-benchmark baseline citations; found only papers using Claude Sonnet 5 as a baseline on unrelated proprietary benchmarks (e.g. a 2026-07-17 arXiv-daily digest entry giving Claude-Sonnet-5 an unrelated agentic Pass@1 of 58.54 on a 1,431-task suite) and general MMMU-Pro methodology papers that do not evaluate this specific model. No paper reports a base-MMMU number for Claude Sonnet 5.

5. GitHub eval-harness / sibling-model cross-reference (combined sweep) -- ran `gh search code "claude-sonnet-5" "mmmu"` (30+ hits reviewed). Found: (a) Sero01/ai-agents-guide's site/src/data/models.json carries a hardcoded "mmmu": 77.0 field for claude-sonnet-5 with zero citation anywhere in the repo, and it is internally inconsistent with this corpus's own verified data -- the same JSON row lists "swe_bench": 72.7 for this model, which contradicts the real, independently-confirmed SWE-bench Verified score of 85.2% already on file here, indicating estimated/templated filler content rather than a real measurement (same rejection pattern already applied elsewhere in this file to best-of-ai/best-of-ai and automatio.ai); rejected on that basis. (b) christicode/benchmark-citations' GAPS.md tracks a "Claude Sonnet 5 -- model_card" data-entry-gap section, but its only tracked blank field for this doc is BrowseComp (already resolved in this corpus) -- it does not address MMMU at all, so it is a non-signal rather than corroboration either way. (c) Sibling-model files in this same corpus (claude-opus-4-8.json, claude-fable-5.json) each carry an 'mmmu' entry sourced to the identical Vals AI mislabeled-Pro tile (86.59% and 89.3% respectively) without flagging the Pro-mislabeling issue that this file's round-1 pass first identified -- this is a known cross-file inconsistency already called out in this file's existing verificationNotes as "flagged separately for a dedicated cross-file fix, out of scope for this claude-sonnet-5.json-only pass," and remains out of scope here; it does not change today's conclusion for claude-sonnet-5.json specifically, since applying the same Vals-AI-mislabeling scrutiny this file already applies would mean none of those sibling entries are genuine base-MMMU scores either.

RESULT: Modality confirmed non-applicable-exclusion (model is vision-capable), so this is treated as a genuine research question, not a skip. Across all five source-method categories (official/model-card, independent trackers, arena/human-preference leaderboards, academic, GitHub/sibling-cross-reference) re-checked today with fresh direct fetches, no credible, correctly-labeled base-MMMU (non-Pro) score exists anywhere for Claude Sonnet 5. The one number that superficially looks like an answer (Vals AI's 83.006% "MMMU" tile) is confirmed, via the tracker's own page title/H1, to actually be MMMU-Pro data under a legacy URL slug, and the one flat numeric hit from a GitHub aggregator (77.0%) is uncited and directly contradicted by this file's own verified SWE-bench figure. This is now a second independent confirmation (round 1 on 2026-07-16, this pass on 2026-07-18) that plain MMMU is a genuine industry-wide non-publication for this model, not a search shortfall -- mmmu is moved to omitted_no_evidence.

2026-07-24 FRONTIERBENCH: ingested the FrontierBench v0.1 leaderboard row (see the result's own notes for harness, effort, CI, tokens and cost).
